search_queries_used: DHA telehealth gastroenteritis in children clinical guideline PDF
population_applicability: Direct for children with acute gastroenteritis; broader or chronic vomiting and diarrhea causes remain outside scope.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact source is limited to DHA-licensed telehealth services.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Version/Issue 2 copy opened on 2026-07-11; document states revision date 2029-02-21.
superseded_check: DHA official telehealth portal checked on 2026-07-11; no newer official issue was identified there.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Defines the exact pediatric Dubai telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines pediatric gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, and instability concern prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Prioritises red flags, dehydration, severe alternate illness, age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, neurological abnormality, and instability.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped acute gastroenteritis terminology and vomiting or diarrhea symptom pattern.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the acute gastroenteritis symptom pattern and limits laboratory studies to atypical or targeted presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped escalation concerns and worsening review without autonomous referral output.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, instability, caregiver, and worsening criteria for face-to-face assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped hydration, activity, consciousness, urine, mucous membrane, capillary refill, turgor, and heart-rate fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists activity, consciousness, thirst, urine output, mucous membranes, pulse, capillary refill, skin turgor, heart rate, and respiratory signs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped stool, vomiting, urine, intake, fever, abdominal pain, medication, and exposure history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies duration, stool and emesis character, urine output, intake, fever, abdominal pain, medication, contact, and exposure history.

unresolved_source_gaps: The source does not cover every cause of pediatric vomiting or diarrhea.
Treatment and feeding choices, fixed follow-up intervals, and school documentation remain unsupported by this mapping.
The workflow is broader than DHA telehealth setting.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
